frozen_components: pre-trained contextual language model (BERT or equivalent)
architecture.summary: Contextualized Construct Representation (CCR) bridges psychometric scales and contextual language models (e.g., BERT) for theory-driven text analysis. The system encodes text using a pre-trained language model, then projects contextualized token representations into a psychometric construct space defined by items from validated psychological scales. Information flows from raw text → contextual embeddings → construct-space projections → psychological outcome predictions. The method retains interpretability by anchoring to explicit psychometric theory while leveraging modern NLP representations.
trained_components: projection from contextual embeddings to psychometric construct space

biblio.title: Contextualized Construct Representation: Leveraging Psychometric Scales to Advance Theory-Driven Text Analysis

claims.statement: CCR outperforms other top-down methods (word-counting and word-embedding representations) in predicting psychological outcomes including moral values, need for cognition, political ideology, strength of norms, and cultural orientation
CCR retains high levels of interpretability and top-down flexibility while making use of state-of-the-art contextual language models
The technique demonstrates effectiveness across two studies with N = 2,996 participants in predicting psychological outcomes common in social and personality psychology
